title: Next Round
description: "Contestant who earns a score equal to or greater than the k-th place finisher's score will advance to the next round, as long as the contestant earns a positive score..." -- anass-backwards rule from some programming contest. In other words, a contestant advances if their score is at least the score of the contestant in k-th place AND their score is positive. Given the scores of all n contestants in non-increasing order, determine how many contestants advance to the next round.
input_spec: The first line contains two integers n and k (1 <= k <= n <= 50). The second line contains n integers a_1, a_2, ..., a_n (0 <= a_i <= 100) -- the scores in non-increasing order.
output_spec: Print a single integer -- the number of contestants who advance to the next round.

routes.name: Threshold comparison
routes.description: Determine the k-th place score, then count how many contestants meet both conditions.

observations.title: Find the threshold score
observations.description: The threshold is the score of the contestant in k-th place. Since the array is 0-indexed, this is a[k-1].

hints.nudge: What score does the k-th place contestant have?
hints.question: The scores are given in non-increasing order. How do you access the k-th place score using 0-based indexing?
hints.formal: threshold = a[k-1]. Any contestant with score >= threshold AND score > 0 advances.

observations.title: Two conditions for advancing
observations.description: A contestant advances if and only if their score is >= the threshold AND their score is strictly positive (> 0).

hints.nudge: There are two conditions a contestant must satisfy. What are they?
hints.question: What happens if the k-th place score is 0? Do contestants with score 0 advance?
hints.formal: advance if score >= threshold and score > 0. The second example shows that if threshold = 0, nobody advances because scores must be positive.

observations.title: Count qualifying contestants
observations.description: Iterate through all scores and count those satisfying both conditions.

hints.nudge: How do you count elements in a list that satisfy a condition?
hints.question: Can you use a simple loop or a list comprehension to count contestants who meet both criteria?
hints.formal: count = sum(1 for x in a if x >= threshold and x > 0). Since the array is sorted, you could also stop early, but a simple count works within the constraints.
